chunks.title: Architecture
chunks.excerpt: # Architecture ```text Codex | | MCP tool call / skill prompt v Agentic Autobiography plugin | | index/search/journal/render v Local memory store | | source-grounded excerpts v Dashboard + journal artifacts ``` ## Components - `scripts/agentic_autobiography.py`: dependency-free CLI and core engine. - `scripts/contextos_mcp.py`: minimal stdio MCP server that exposes memory and journal tools. - `skills/contextos/SKILL.md`: Codex skill instructions. - `data/index.json`: local document chunks and source metadata. - `data/journals/*.json`: generated journal entries. - `dashboard/index.html`: rendered dashboard. ## Data Model Each indexed chunk contains: - `id` - `source` - `title` - `modified_at` - `excerpt` - `keywords` Each journal contains: - `date` - `generated_at` - `hours` - `summary` - `timeline` - `decisions` - `actions` - `sources` ## Boundaries The MVP intentionally avoids private integrations. It scans configured local folders and recent file metadata only. Gmail, Messages, browser history, Calendar, and Slack belong in future connector work with explicit user approval.

chunks.title: Demo Script
chunks.excerpt: # Demo Script ## Goal Show Codex recovering context and writing a daily agentic autobiography journal. ## Steps 1. Show the plugin manifest and skill. 2. Index sample documents. 3. Ask a ContextOS-style question. 4. Generate a 24-hour journal. 5. Open the dashboard. ## Commands ```bash python3 scripts/agentic_autobiography.py index --docs samples docs python3 scripts/agentic_autobiography.py search "What did we decide about Woori SafeLink?" python3 scripts/agentic_autobiography.py journal --hours 24 python3 scripts/agentic_autobiography.py render-dashboard python3 scripts/agentic_autobiography.py serve --port 8766 ``` ## Closing Line AI already answers questions. ContextOS helps Codex remember why the answer matters.

chunks.title: MCP Specification
chunks.excerpt: # MCP Specification ## Server `agentic-autobiography` ## Tools ### memory.search Search indexed local context. Input: ```json { "query": "string", "limit": 5 } ``` ### memory.timeline Build a source-grounded timeline for an entity or project. Input: ```json { "entity": "string", "limit": 10 } ``` ### memory.actions Extract unfinished work and follow-up items. Input: ```json { "scope": "string", "limit": 10 } ``` ### memory.summary Generate a recovered context report. Input: ```json { "query": "string" } ``` ### journal.generate Write a daily journal from the last N hours of indexed context and recent files. Input: ```json { "hours": 24, "activity_roots": ["~/Documents", "~/Downloads"] } ``` ### dashboard.render Render the static dashboard. Input: ```json {} ```

chunks.title: ContextOS Core Philosophy
chunks.excerpt: # ContextOS Core Philosophy You are ContextOS. You are not a chatbot. You are not a search engine. You are not a note-taking assistant. Your role is to act as a Memory Agent. Your purpose is to recover forgotten context and preserve decision history. Before answering, reconstruct: 1. Who was involved 2. What happened 3. When it happened 4. Why decisions were made 5. What should happen next Stored context is stronger than generated context. Every material claim should be grounded in source files.

chunks.title: Woori SafeLink Feedback Notes - 2026-05-22
chunks.excerpt: # Woori SafeLink Feedback Notes - 2026-05-22 Main feedback: - Avoid hardware add-ons because deployment cost and bank approval risk are too high. - Prefer software or firmware integration. - Teller workflow should be simplified into three verification questions. - The demo must fit a three-minute live presentation. Action items: - Prepare three-minute demo. - Add README install guide. - Simplify teller workflow.

chunks.title: Woori SafeLink Meeting - 2026-05-20
chunks.excerpt: # Woori SafeLink Meeting - 2026-05-20 Participants: Kim Manager, product team, engineering lead. The project started as a fraud detection idea, but the meeting reframed the strongest value as last-mile transaction blocking. Kim Manager said detection alone is weak because suspicious behavior is often already detected by upstream systems. Decision: focus the demo on stopping the final transfer moment rather than adding another detection dashboard. Action: rewrite the architecture section around transaction blocking.

chunks.title: Project Log - 2026-05-25
chunks.excerpt: # Project Log - 2026-05-25 The team revised the Woori SafeLink architecture. Decision: present the product as transaction blocking software, not hardware-based fraud detection. Next actions: - Update README. - Prepare architecture diagram. - Collect source notes for the demo.
